title: SEC4: has_id double-null check in transport.rs
description: transport.rs line 82: `let has_id = \!value["id"].is_null() && value["id"] \!= Value::Null;` checks the same condition twice. `Value::Null` is exactly what `is_null()` tests, so the second clause is always redundant. A maintainer who sees this will either spend time convincing themselves it matters, or assume there is a subtle distinction between the two forms (there isn't). Fix: simplify to `let has_id = \!value["id"].is_null();` with a comment: // JSON-RPC: id is present and non-null (null id is not valid per spec, but guard anyway)
close_reason: Fixed in nie-7bv6.24 fix wave; code now uses value.get('id').is_some()
